title: rubus hirtus var. uncinatus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. uncinatus, also known as the thimbleberry or bristly raspberry, is a type of shrub native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its edible red fruit and thorny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and fight infections. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus var. uncinatus include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and internally to support overall health and well-being.
